title: 4 things to know about new Patriots edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Dre'Mont Jones joins the Patriots after playing for Ohio State, Broncos, Seahawks, Titans, and Ravens in recent years.
Jones recorded 15 quarterback hits in nine games with the Ravens, showcasing his pass-rushing abilities.
Standing at 6-foot-3, 281 pounds, Jones offers versatility, able to play as an edge rusher or defensive tackle.

title: Seamark at Encore Boston to close, less than a month after workers unionize
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Seamark Seafood & Cocktails at Encore Boston Harbor will close on March 29, ending operations amid economic challenges.
The closure comes weeks after 56 Seamark employees voted to unionize, leading to claims of retaliatory motives from the union.
UNITE HERE Local 26 hopes Encore takes over Seamark and retains workers; responses from Carver Hospitality and Encore awaited.

title: A nearly 100-year-old Catholic school in Medford is closing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: St. Joseph School in Medford will close after 97 years due to declining enrollment and rising operational costs.
Current enrollment is 169 students, down from 222 in the 2021-2022 school year, impacting sustainability.
Tuition assistance and faculty prioritization will support transitions to other Catholic schools in the area.

title: This 14-time marathoner is running the Boston Marathon for his daughter and children’s health
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Chris Ruggio is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with the Boston Children's Hospital Miles for Miracles team.
Ruggio's daughter, Holly, has juvenile idiopathic arthritis and has received care at Boston Children's Hospital.
Ruggio has raised over $70,000 for children's health charities through his marathon efforts.

title: A 15-year-old girl was found dead in a Plymouth forest in 1986. Her alleged killer is finally standing trial.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Michael Hand, 69, stands trial for the alleged 1986 murder of 15-year-old Tracy Gilpin in Plymouth, Massachusetts.
Gilpin was found dead with skull fractures and a blood alcohol level of 0.20 in Myles Standish State Forest.
Hand was arrested in 2018 in North Carolina and has pleaded not guilty to first-degree murder charges.

title: Healey demands $1,745 tariff refunds for each Mass. household
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey demands $1,745 tariff refunds per household in Massachusetts, totaling nearly $4.9 billion.
The demand follows the Supreme Court's decision striking down tariffs imposed under former President Trump's emergency powers.
Massachusetts, part of a 24-state coalition, sued against new 15% tariffs amid concerns of rising consumer costs.

title: Mattapan store owner pleads guilty in $6.7M SNAP fraud scheme
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mattapan store owner Antonio Bonheur pleaded guilty to $6.7 million in fraudulent SNAP transactions over several years.
Bonheur admitted to wire fraud and unauthorized SNAP benefit use at Jesula Variety Store, exchanging benefits for cash and ineligible items.
Co-defendant Saul Alisme faces charges for trafficking over $121,000 in SNAP benefits; his trial is delayed until mid-May.

title: JetBlue is cutting a route between Boston and this southern city
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue is discontinuing a seasonal route between Boston and Asheville, North Carolina, due to unmet performance expectations.
The Boston-Asheville route, launched in 2022, will not resume as planned in May; affected customers receive full refunds.
JetBlue continues to focus on sustainable routes and was named one of the safest low-cost airlines for 2026.

title: Theater review: ‘Stereophonic’ at Emerson Colonial really makes you feel like you’re with the band
author: Jackie Chianca
bullets: 'Stereophonic' features an immersive production at Emerson Colonial Theatre, capturing the artistic experience with a set in 1976.
Will Butler of Arcade Fire composed original songs, enhancing the play's portrayal of a rock band's recording process.
Directed by Daniel Aukin, the play examines character dynamics and emotional tension among band members and sound engineers.

title: Joe Mazzulla referenced his high school principal while defending Jaylen Brown after ejection
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics' Jaylen Brown ejected after two quick technical fouls in a loss to the Spurs.
Coach Joe Mazzulla defended Brown, criticizing the decision of official Suyash Mehta to eject him.
Celtics face defending NBA champion Thunder on Thursday at 9:30 p.m. in Oklahoma City.
